items.title: Reasons released for Butters AFL Tribunal decision

children.content: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

articles.title: Player's umpire abuse verdict raises questions about AFL's 'conflict of interest'

children.content: The reporting of Port Adelaide player Zak Butters for umpire abuse sparks debate about whether officials should be involved with betting and if better audio recordings should be available from games.

image.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

articles.title: Butters found guilty of abusing umpire as Port claims 'glaring hole in the evidence'

children.content: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters is found guilty of umpire abuse at the AFL Tribunal, despite strongly denying allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?".

image.altText: Zak Butters speaks into microphones

articles.title: Butters asked 'why did you pay that?' Koch says

children.content: Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says the AFL club will back Zak Butters "to the hilt" amid dispute over what was said to an umpire during Sunday night's loss against St Kilda.

image.altText: AFL player Zak Butters at a media conference.

gallery.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

thumbnail.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

items.title: 'Calculated' theft of $12,000 worth of fuel prompts arrest

children.content: A 36-year-old man has been charged with theft amid an investigation into as many as 20 petrol drive-offs, which police say involved an SUV that contained a tank used to carry stolen diesel.

image.altText: The back of a car with its boot open.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as fuel costs hit tourism

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

image.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

items.title: Teen admits creating deepfakes in Australian-first prosecution

children.content: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

gallery.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

thumbnail.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Man says he was acting in 'self-defence' when he kicked a dog twice in the head

children.content: A northern suburbs man says he had been bitten and was acting in self-defence when he kicked a dog that was tethered to the tray of a ute in a Bunnings car park.

image.altText: Maya was allegedly kicked to the head while in a Bunnings car park

items.title: 'Fierce and defiant' disability advocate remembered after drowning

children.content: Ellen Fraser-Barbour is being remembered for her tireless advocacy and love of nature after her death at the age of 35.

image.altText: A disability advocate with her friend.

items.title: 'She was extraordinary': Famed sculptor Pat Moseley remembered

children.content: Across dusty outback tracks to the promenades of Adelaide, sculptor Pat Moseley turned limestone and bronze into the faces of legends.

image.altText: black and white photo of artist with horse sculptors

items.title: Billionaire's 'slow moving car crash' leads to individuals being investigated by ASIC

children.content: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

image.altText: GFG Alliance executive chair Sanjeev Gupta.

items.title: Blow to cartography in Australia as The Map Shop prepares to close

children.content: One of Australia's few remaining cartography shops is about to close in Adelaide, prompting questions about where people can buy the critical documents, particularly those travelling in remote areas where mobile coverage is limited.

gallery.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps

thumbnail.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps

items.title: Most truckies fear they won't last 12 months, industry survey says

children.content: Trucking industry peak group NatRoad calls on the federal government to urgently implement the $1 billion Economic Resilience Program it promised two weeks ago.

image.altText: A large blue truck with cattle transporting trailers attached in a small country town.

items.title: Qantas cancels flights between Adelaide and Mount Gambier amid fuel costs

children.content: Qantas will no longer fly to South Australia's largest regional city, citing fuel costs and declining demand for flights to Mount Gambier.

image.altText: Qantas Mount Gambier

items.title: Residents near cement plant raise concerns about more plastic being burnt for fuel

children.content: Residents living near a large cement plant in Adelaide are concerned about the owner wanting to burn more plastic as fuel to replace natural gas.

gallery.altText: aerial image of a manufacturing plant with river to the right and cloudy sky in background

thumbnail.altText: aerial image of a manufacturing plant with river to the right and cloudy sky in background

items.title: Rate rises, spending cuts and cost blowouts: Councils squeezed by fuel crisis

children.content: Some South Australian councils may hike rates above inflation this year as they grapple with cost blowouts, spending cuts and project delays caused by the fuel crisis.

image.altText: two diggers in a fenced off area with flattened dirt next to a road with cars passing by

items.title: One Nation approaches independent MPs as it makes the case to be SA's opposition

children.content: One Nation SA leader Cory Bernardi has approached independent MPs to make the case for his party — instead of the Liberals — to be recognised as the state's official opposition.

image.altText: composite of Matt Schultz, Lou Nicholson and Geoff Brock

items.title: Reptile remains believed to be a crocodile found dumped on side of road

children.content: Reptile remains — which are believed to be from a crocodile — have been collected by authorities after they were found dumped on the side of an Adelaide road.

image.altText: A crocodile in brown water looking straight on at the camera close-up like a portrait.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade could turn into global agrifood crisis

children.content: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations says ships in the Strait of Hormuz need to start moving again to avoid a global agrifood crisis. Here's why.

image.altText: Green wheat in focus in the foreground with blurred mature wheat in the background

images.altText: a us navy destroyer ship moving right to left in the ocean

children.content: The talks come as both the US and Iran continue to impose their own separate restrictions on the flow of vessels in and out of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: Trump says war is 'very close to over' as mediators push ceasefire extension

updates.title: Trump says he views Iran war 'as very close to over'
Iran's top general warns US over naval blockade

items.title: Lesbian Action Group wins appeal in case seeking to ban trans women

children.content: The Federal Court allows a lesbian action group's appeal to be allowed to legally exclude transgender women from its public events.

image.altText: Lesbian Action Group celebrate the win outside court

items.title: Hotel owner looks to sue Australia for $11m over unpaid refugee bills

children.content: A hotel in Papua New Guinea is attempting to sue the Australian government over accommodation costs for refugees previously detained on Manus Island.

image.altText: A Papua New Guinean man with a moustache sits in a chair wearing a button up shirt.

items.title: Teen admits creating deepfakes in Australian-first prosecution

children.content: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

image.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

items.title: Media boss's wife feared she would die, tore his clothing during alleged attack, court told

children.content: The wife of media executive Antony Catalano fled the couple's luxury apartment and flagged down a passing car for help following a sustained attack by her husband that caused hospitalisation, police allege.

image.altText: A side profile shot of Australian Community Media executive chairman Antony Catalano in an outdoor setting.

items.title: 'Your courage does not end': Prince Harry mingles with veterans in solo Canberra trip

children.content: The Duke of Sussex was in the nation's capital without his wife Meghan and addressed an Invictus Australia event.

gallery.altText: Prince Harry attends an Invictus Australia reception at the Aust

thumbnail.altText: Prince Harry attends an Invictus Australia reception at the Aust
Prince Harry lays a wreath at the Australian War Memorial
Crowd at War Memorial for Prince Harry visit

items.title: Cage fighter was 'mucking around' when spar turned deadly, jury hears

children.content: Jurors have heard that the accused's girlfriend suggested he call an ambulance after the sparring session, but he told her to "hurry up and just get here".

image.altText: a forensic officer wearing a hazmat suit takes pictures at a mount druitt home after an alleged murder

items.title: Which shade of white is right? Dentists explain the teeth colours

children.content: Experts say there are many factors that influence how white a tooth appears, and it's not just about whitening or what we’re eating.

image.altText: A close up image of a woman looking at her teeth in a small mirror

items.title: How the technology we use every day is changing our brains

children.content: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

gallery.altText: An illustration of an abstract human brain, using coloured paper cut-outs against a blue paper background.

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of an abstract human brain, using coloured paper cut-outs against a blue paper background.

items.title: How The Babadook inspired the 'scariest movie you'll ever hear'

children.content: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

image.altText: A woman in a dimly lit room screams

items.title: Jury hears 'no bad blood' before sistergirl's alleged fatal stabbing

children.content: Jurors in the murder trial of a man accused of fatally stabbing a 37-year-old sistergirl from the Tiwi Islands have heard there was "no bad blood" between them in the lead-up to the "family tragedy".

image.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

items.title: School teacher who broke student's teeth found guilty of causing serious harm

children.content: A former Darwin Middle School teacher who struck a female student in the face, breaking her teeth, after she demanded her confiscated phone back has been found guilty by a jury of unlawfully causing serious harm.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

items.title: Gold mine stops underground operations after magnitude-4.5 earthquake

children.content: All underground workers at Newmont's Cadia gold mine have been brought to the surface as inspections are carried out following an earthquake.

image.altText: Satellite map image with yellow dots of earthquake locations..

items.title: Brothers living in squalor missed multiple health appointments, report finds

children.content: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.

gallery.altText: a composite image of two boys, seen from behind
a squalid bedroom with a sheet over the window
a window in a house looking out positioned on a dirty and damaged wall

thumbnail.altText: a composite image of two boys, seen from behind
a squalid bedroom with a sheet over the window
a window in a house looking out positioned on a dirty and damaged wall

items.title: Exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback

children.content: Projects are in the works to expand Australia's domestic fertiliser supply as prices rise by 60 per cent.

image.altText: A hand holds brilliant white fertiliser while a truck tips it off in the background

items.title: Workers at embattled smelter face last payday amid calls for certainty

children.content: As what could be their last payday looms, an alliance of unions says more should be done to ensure stability for 200 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter.

image.altText: A steel worker in highly protective gear working on hot steel products.

items.title: Experts tip further flight disruptions as Virgin, Qantas trim domestic capacity

children.content: Australian airlines are halting entire routes and merging slots to ensure planes are full, as the aviation industry battles soaring jet fuel costs and likely supply uncertainty over the coming months.

image.altText: A virgin plane taking off into the sky.

items.title: Man cleared of stalking Israeli President Herzog during Sydney trip

children.content: Rajab Ali was arrested in February while President Isaac Herzog was in Sydney to pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: A man with a moustache and beard looking at the camera inside a room.

items.title: New footage captures moment Artemis II crew welcomed home

children.content: New footage shows the moment a recovery crew opens the hatch of Artemis II's Orion capsule and welcomes its crew members home after their mission around the Moon.

image.altText: Crew opening capsule.

gallery.altText: Two astronauts in orange space suits are seen sitting in a space capsule. One of them is giving a thumbs up sign.

thumbnail.altText: Two astronauts in orange space suits are seen sitting in a space capsule. One of them is giving a thumbs up sign.
